patches.summary: Gate limit/cursor query params to resources whose list endpoint accepts them (tags, tasks) and stop sending an incremental 'since' param, which no Morgen list endpoint accepts.
patches.reason: The v4.16.0 generator applied uniform pagination (limit on every list endpoint) plus a 'since' param to all resources. Morgen strictly whitelists query params and returns HTTP 400 for unknown ones, so sync failed on /calendars/list (limit) and /tags/list (since).
patches.validated_outcome: Live dogfood sync 4xx failures cleared; /tags/list?limit=100 returns HTTP 200; calendars/integrations/events no longer receive limit.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Per-resource pagination gating (resourceSupportsPagination / syncResourceSinceParam) is already emitted by Printing Press v4.24.0 templates; a clean reprint on >=4.24.0 supersedes this hand patch.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Generator template bug present in v4.16.0, fixed upstream. This artifact stays v4.16.0-printed because a clean reprint must also re-carry the novel 'agenda' command.

patches.summary: Sort param keys before hashing the response-cache key so the same logical request always maps to one cache entry.
patches.reason: The generated cacheKey iterated the params map in Go's randomized order, so multi-param reads (e.g. the agenda command's /events/list call) produced a different SHA each call, making the cache a no-op and forcing an extra live API hit every time.

patches.summary: ClearTokens now also clears MorgenApiKey and AuthHeaderVal so 'auth logout' actually de-authenticates; also drops a redundant duplicate empty-key guard in AuthHeader.
patches.reason: The generated ClearTokens cleared only OAuth bearer fields, leaving the api_key credential persisted; AuthHeader still returned a valid 'ApiKey ...' header after logout, so users believed they were logged out while still authenticated.

patches.summary: tail honors --follow=false by returning after the initial poll instead of always entering the infinite ticker loop.
patches.reason: The generated tail command read the follow flag into a variable but never checked it, so --follow=false (documented as a single poll) looped forever.

patches.summary: Route sync/tail streamed NDJSON through cmd.OutOrStdout() (sync via a mutex-guarded writer for its concurrent workers) so --deliver tees the stream to its sink instead of producing an empty file.
patches.reason: sync and tail wrote events directly to os.Stdout, bypassing the cobra writer the --deliver MultiWriter tees; --deliver file:... silently produced an empty file.
patches.validated_outcome: morgen-pp-cli sync --json --deliver file:out.json now writes populated NDJSON (verified live); go build/vet clean.
